description: Baseline: added autoresearch harness using two CI-like unit-suite reruns in autoresearch.sh and docs/typecheck/test:stable gating in autoresearch.checks.sh. Current baseline is green with ci_failure_score=0.

Retargeted autoresearch to the real PR #61 CI failure: repeated CI-like reruns of the JSON-RPC thread/resume replay timeout. Baseline reproduced 0 failures across 6 focused reruns, and docs/typecheck/full CI-style unit suite/test:stable all passed.

Widened the reproducer from the single PR-failing test to the entire test/server.jsonrpc.flow.test.ts file under CI=true. Baseline stayed green across 8 reruns, with full docs/typecheck/CI-style suite/test:stable checks still passing.

Switched the benchmark to the actual PR #61 merge ref in a worktree and ran the real CI unit suite twice under CI=true. Both benchmark runs passed, but the correctness gate later reproduced the exact flake: server JSON-RPC flows > thread/resume replays a journal cursor once before reattaching the live thread sink timed out after 45s on the merge ref.

Strengthened merge-ref reproduction to 4 repeated CI=true full-suite runs in the actual PR #61 merge worktree. All 4 benchmark runs passed and the merge-ref docs/typecheck/CI-suite/test:stable checks also passed, so the flake is real but rarer than the earlier failed check suggested.

Established a sensitive reproducer for the real flake by hammering the exact PR #61 merge-ref thread/resume JSON-RPC test 200 times under CI=true. Baseline reproduced 17 failures, confirming the race lives in the targeted test path itself rather than needing the full suite.
Pivoted the flake benchmark onto the current repo so candidate fixes can be measured directly. Baseline reproduced 1 timeout in 100 CI-like reruns of the exact thread/resume JSON-RPC test.

Tried hardening the reconnect tests by waiting for the original websocket to fully close before reconnecting. The 100-run stress metric stayed flat at 1 failure, so close-handshake timing alone does not explain the flake.

Added per-step failure diagnostics to the flaky thread/resume test, but the stress metric worsened to 3/100 from added overhead and Bun's test-timeout path still did not flush the custom diagnostics. This path is not giving actionable signal.

Forced active connection shutdown in the flaky test cleanup (`await server.stop(true)`) so Bun does not hang on lingering websocket state. This improved the 100-run thread/resume stress metric from 1 failure to 0, and docs/typecheck/full CI-style suite/test:stable all passed.

Confirmation rerun on the kept `server.stop(true)` cleanup change stayed at 0 failures across another 100 stress iterations with checks still green. No additional improvement versus the kept result, but this increases confidence the fix is real.

Hardened the main CI workflow with explicit typecheck and stable per-file unit test steps, and added test/ci.workflow.test.ts to lock those guardrails in. The workflow benchmark is now green and full docs/typecheck/CI-style suite/test:stable checks all passed.

Codified forced-close teardown guardrails for the websocket-heavy JSON-RPC flow spec by adding stopTestServer() in test/helpers/wsHarness.ts and replacing plain await server.stop() callsites. The unsafe teardown count dropped from 20 to 0, and docs/typecheck/full CI-style suite/test:stable all passed.

Retargeted the benchmark to the remaining websocket-heavy sibling file, test/server.toolstream.test.ts. Baseline found 2 plain await server.stop() teardown callsites still using the known-risk pattern.

Applied the shared forced-close helper to test/server.toolstream.test.ts, replacing its 2 remaining plain await server.stop() teardown callsites. The unsafe toolstream teardown count dropped from 2 to 0, and docs/typecheck/full CI-style suite/test:stable all passed.

Retargeted the benchmark to the remaining server/websocket specs still using plain stop teardowns. Baseline found 48 plain server.stop() callsites across desktop.controlSocket.threadList, harness.ws.e2e, server.jsonrpc.control, server.jsonrpc, and server.test.

Applied stopTestServer() across the remaining server/websocket specs: desktop.controlSocket.threadList, harness.ws.e2e, server.jsonrpc.control, server.jsonrpc, and server.test. The unsafe server teardown count dropped from 48 to 0, and docs/typecheck/full CI-style suite/test:stable all passed.
Broadened the benchmark to all remaining plain server.stop() callsites across test/**/*.test.ts. Baseline found 2 leftovers in the live/persisted handoff test inside test/server.jsonrpc.flow.test.ts.

Removed the final 2 plain server.stop() callsites in test/server.jsonrpc.flow.test.ts by routing the live/persisted handoff teardown through stopTestServer(). The all-tests unsafe teardown count dropped from 2 to 0, and docs/typecheck/full CI-style suite/test:stable all passed.

Retargeted to the real overall signal: 5 repeated CI=true full-suite runs after the teardown and workflow hardening. All 5 benchmark runs passed, and docs/typecheck/CI-style suite/test:stable checks also passed.

Retargeted to the stricter per-file stable runner signal. Three repeated `bun run test:stable -- --max-concurrency 1` passes succeeded, and docs/typecheck/full CI-style suite/test:stable checks also passed.

Confirmation rerun on the repeated stable-runner workload stayed clean at 0 failures across another 3 isolated per-file passes, increasing confidence that the current test suite state is genuinely stable rather than a one-off green run.

Retargeted from flake detection to CI signal quality. Baseline on one full CI-style suite pass shows 41 noisy green-run lines: 29 repeated Google GenAI experimental warnings, 11 repeated Langfuse degraded-config warnings, and 1 expected desktop init error line.

Reduced green-run CI noise with low-risk test-only changes: disabled observability in the shared websocket test helper and silenced the expected desktop init error log in bootstrap-cache.test.ts. Noise dropped from 41 to 32 lines, removing the expected error line and cutting Langfuse warnings from 11 to 3.

Cached the Google Interactions client per API key in src/runtime/googleNativeInteractions.ts and added a focused runtime test proving the SDK experimental warning only fires once per key. Full-suite green-run noise dropped from 32 to 4 lines, with Google warnings reduced from 29 to 1 and checks still all green.
